error.signature: OSError: Error no file named model.safetensors found in directory or unable to load safetensors file

verdict.summary: Failed to load model weights in safetensors format. File missing, corrupted download, or format incompatibility.

dead_ends.action: Convert pickle checkpoint to safetensors manually
dead_ends.why_fails: Manual conversion can introduce errors with custom architectures. Use the official conversion tool or download the correct format.

dead_ends.action: Ignore safetensors and always use use_safetensors=False
dead_ends.why_fails: Pickle files have arbitrary code execution risk. Safetensors is the safe default; fix the load issue instead.

workarounds.action: Force re-download by clearing the cache
Fall back to PyTorch .bin format if safetensors unavailable
workarounds.how: model = AutoModel.from_pretrained('model', use_safetensors=False)  # loads pytorch_model.bin instead

workarounds.action: Verify file integrity with sha256 checksum
workarounds.how: sha256sum model.safetensors  # compare with checksum on the model card or Hub API
